expectations.evidence: Says 'ready to be picked up' but doesn't explicitly offer — informational, not proactive

notes: Eval 1 (add-item) passes 100% in both configs — the baseline peeked at eval fixture files to infer the schema, making it non-discriminating
Eval 2 (show-and-reorder) is the clearest differentiator: skill grouped by status, baseline used flat table
Eval 4 (check-next) shows the proactive behavior difference: skill explicitly offers to start, baseline is informational
With-skill runs are more consistent (0 stddev on pass_rate) and faster on average despite baseline being simpler
Baseline eval 1 used 3x more tool calls (18 vs 6) because it had to search for schema — skill provides this knowledge upfront
